title: HeadObject, GetObject, Conditions, Ranges, and Cleanup · Execution Trace: Get Body Lifecycle
summary: A download service calls GET to discover metadata, leaks response bodies, retries after sending headers, and returns full private objects for malformed ranges. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the get body lifecycle increment in bounded S3 download decision and cleanup flow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Check response and metadata, own and close the body, propagate cancellation, handle partial reads, and distinguish transport from caller writes.
Choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Get Body Lifecycle · Theory Get Body Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8148 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove S3 response-body cleanup; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
